cards.text: The copy assignment operator has signature: ClassName{{c1::&}} operator{{c2::=}}(const ClassName{{c3::&}} rhs);
operator= must be a {{c1::member function}} and should return {{c2::*this}} by {{c3::reference}} to allow chaining: a = b = c.

cards.question: What does this assignment operator do?
cards.answer: Copy assignment: checks for self-assignment, deletes old data, allocates new memory, copies rhs data, returns *this.
cards.explanation: Standard pattern for deep copy assignment with dynamic memory.

cards.front: Why must you check for self-assignment in operator=?
cards.back: Self-assignment (a = a) would delete a's data before copying it, causing undefined behavior. The check if (this == &rhs) return *this; prevents this. Without it, you'd delete the source data you're trying to copy.

cards.text: The self-assignment check is: if ({{c1::this}} {{c2::==}} {{c3::&rhs}}) return *this;

cards.front: What's the difference between copy assignment and copy constructor?
cards.back: Copy constructor initializes a new object from an existing one (MyClass b = a;). Copy assignment copies to an already-existing object (b = a;). Assignment must clean up existing resources first.

cards.text: Copy assignment pattern: check {{c1::self-assignment}}, {{c2::clean up}} old resources, {{c3::copy}} new data, return {{c4::*this}}.

cards.front: Why does operator= return *this by reference?
cards.back: To enable assignment chaining (a = b = c) and match built-in behavior. The return value of (b = c) is b, which then gets assigned to a. Reference avoids unnecessary copying.

cards.question: What's wrong with this operator=?
cards.answer: Missing self-assignment check. If rhs is *this, deleting m_data destroys the source before copying.
cards.explanation: Must add: if (this == &rhs) return *this; at the beginning.

cards.front: What does the compiler-generated default operator= do?
cards.back: It performs memberwise assignment: copies each member variable from rhs to *this using their assignment operators. This does shallow copy, which is problematic for classes managing resources.

cards.explanation:
Essential steps for operator=:
  • Check for self-assignment (this == &rhs)
  • Clean up any existing resources (*this already exists)
  • Copy all data from rhs
  • Return *this by reference
  • Must be a member function
  • Handle exceptions safely (use copy-and-swap idiom)

cards.text: The parameter to operator= should be {{c1::const ClassName&}} to prevent modifying the source and avoid {{c2::unnecessary copies}}.
